description:
Update README.md and SKILL.md to cover the new lifecycle surface:

- README.md: new top-level section "Delivery Modes" between "Architecture: the state branch" and the "Task Schema" sections. Cover local-squash (today's default) and deferred (forge-gated). Wire to docs/SPEC-forge-gated-delivery.md. Document the implicit "current branch at repo root = integration target" as the fallback when target_branch is unset, since that has lived undocumented in review.rs.
- README.md: add target_branch to the config section.
- README.md: add a forge-plugin row to the GitHub Issues / Jira / Linear comparison table or its own bullet — these are different plugins (issue tracker vs forge), and the docs should make that crisp so users don't try to use balls-plugin-github for issue tracking.
- SKILL.md: new section "Forge-gated review (opt-in)" before "Multi-agent: split submitter and reviewer". Cover what changes for an agent: bl review pushes the branch instead of squashing, the auto-gate child appears, what to do with it (don't claim it), how the close eventually unblocks.
- SKILL.md: document the BC caveat (old bl on a deferred-mode repo running bl review will still local-squash) in plain agent-facing language. Suggest checking min_bl_version.
- Update the "Task Lifecycle" diagram to show the deferred-mode branch of the in_progress→review transition.

WHY: existing docs implicitly assume trunk-based with no external gate. SKILL.md's "Multi-agent" section is about two local agents, not external reviewers via a forge — and the README's only mention of forge PRs is the release-plz section, which uses forge PRs *out of band* from balls. Users in git-flow or forge-gated repos have no way to discover today that balls can support their flow at all (and post-impl, they need to know how).

ACCEPTANCE:
- README and SKILL both reference the new SPEC file.
- Worked example in README for both delivery modes (single-agent trunk; agent + forge PR).
- BC caveat appears in both docs.
title: Docs: delivery modes, target_branch, and forge-gated flow
